books.title: Why Buildings Stand Up
books.author: Mario Salvadori
books.rationale: Used to give a solid and interesting introduction to building principles.  The history of architecture is explored along with the technology that explains why the buildings stand up. The author is passionate about his subject and desires his readers to love beautiful buildings, too.  He writes about architecture as the successful marriage of art and technology.

books.title: 50 Buildings You Should Know
books.author: Isabel Kuhl
books.rationale: This book is used for several history rotations to study some of the fascinating buildings of the time period. For this rotation, there are only 2 lessons from this book, so we have made the book optional.  No need to purchase the book just for this rotation.

books.title: Drawing, Design, and Craft-Work
books.author: Frederick J. Glass
books.rationale: This is an excellent guide to art instruction, with suggestions for how to begin, materials to use, and the directions future work may take. This book is profusely illustrated with clear explanations.

books.author: Betty Edwards
books.rationale: This is an optional resource for students who would like a resource guide for color theory and/or would want to learn more than what will be covered in lessons.

books.title: Brushwork: Elementary Brush-Forms
books.author: Marion Hudson
books.rationale: This book is perfect for those who are new to brush drawing. The exercises on gridded paper help students learn to mix colors and control the brush. Brushforms ("blobs") are used to create designs.
books.sharedText:
Art: Level 1
Art: Level 2
Art: Level 3

books.title: Brush Drawing as Applied to Natural Forms and Common Objects
books.author: May Mallam
books.rationale: This is a complete guide to learning how to brush-draw. Exercises progress from simple lines to monochromatic value painting to painting in color. Techniques taught help students learn to "see" so that they can visually represent their observations.
books.sharedText:
Art: Level 4
Art: Level 5
Art: Level 6
Art: Level 7
Art: High School Fast Track

books.title: Redeeming Vision
books.author: Elissa Weichbrodt
books.rationale: Educators of all levels and high school students take the next step in deepening their relationship with art by contemplating a theology of art for viewers which impacts how we see as well as how we are and how we love.  Practice in taking what has been learned in previous years along with new aspects of art elements and themes is guided by Dr. Weichbrodt.

books.title: Art Print Set
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This art print set contains high-resolution images that are in the public domain or used for Alveary with permission. The Art Print Set contains paintings by each term's artist: Edward Mitchell Bannister, Vincent van Gogh, and Lilias Trotter. We recommend printing on cardstock. Hard copies will be available with lesson plans.

books.title: Our Work: Grades 1-2
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This is a compilation of the year's Recitation, Picture Study, Hymns, Spirituals, and Folk Songs. Line drawings of artifacts from the historical time period are also included to provide students with inspiration for their Book of Centuries. We recommend printing or purchasing a copy for each student.

books.title: A Passion for the Impossible: The Life of Lilias Trotter
books.author: Miriam Rockness
books.rationale: An inspiring biography of Lilias Trotter, a British artist who forsook fame to devote her life to Christian missionary work in Algeria, guided by faith, creativity, and love.

books.title: Van Gogh
books.author: Ingo Walther & Rainer Metzger
books.rationale: This comprehensive study of Vincent van Gogh (1853–1890) pairs a detailed monograph on his life and art with a complete catalogue of his 871 paintings.

books.title: The Arts: A Visual Encyclopedia
books.author: DK
books.rationale: We will be using this as our spine throughout the course. It is a nice overview of global art history. This book was chosen due to its miniscule amount of nudity compared to other art books geared to teens.

books.title: Children's Book of Art
books.author: DK
books.rationale: This is a nice overview of global art history with some different pieces shown and discussed. It also has close-up sections explaining how to do things in art such as mixing colors or making a fresco, etc. This will be referenced throughout the course for different techniques and extra examples of genres. However, this will be a second resource and all topics will be taught in a way that students can see images online if they do not have the book on hand. This book was chosen due to its minuscule amount of nudity compared to other art books geared to teens.

books.title: Old Testament Today
books.author: John H. Walton & Andrew E. Hill
books.rationale: It is so important for students to understand the context in which the Bible was written and what it meant to the original audience. However, it is also important to understand how the OT writings are relevant to us today. The authors do a wonderful job communicating all these ideas. Lesson plans will guide you in navigating this rich text.

books.title: NIV Cultural Backgrounds Study Bible: Bringing to Life the Ancient World of Scripture
books.author: Zondervan
books.rationale: Contains unabridged Scripture along with important archaeological, historical, and cultural background.
books.sharedText:
Old Testament: Grades 7-12
New Testament: Grade 9
New Testament: Grades 10-11
New Testament: Grade 12

books.title: ESV Concise Study Bible
books.rationale: A smaller-sized study Bible that is appropriate for this age. We highly suggest this Bible for the insightful study notes and accessibility for this age group. During lesson time, students will be asked to review the study notes and other explanatory material.
books.sharedText:
Old Testament: Grades 7-12
New Testament: Grades 7-8

books.title: The Student Bible Atlas, Revised Edition
books.author: Tim Dowley
books.rationale: This is a simple, inexpensive Bible atlas that provides full-page maps of the biblical lands of the Old and New Testaments. In Bible lessons, students will be directed to look at specific pages. This resource is for Grades 3-8 ONLY.
books.sharedText:
Old Testament: Grades 3-6
New Testament: Grades 3-6

books.title: Introducing the New Testament: A Historical, Literary, and Theological Survey
books.author: Mark Allan Powell
books.rationale: An overview of the NT for older students and educators providing book by book background and major themes to be used for years. The chapters can be read in any order allowing for use anywhere in the reading rotation. Powell presents various perspectives on interpretive issues to encourage engagement with ideas but does not attempt to resolve all the issues.  Artwork through the centuries adds a unique element that illustrates themes and demonstrates the influence of the NT over the centuries.
books.sharedText:
New Testament: Grades 7-8
New Testament: Grade 9
New Testament: Grades 10-11
New Testament: Grade 12

books.title: Luke for Everyone
books.author: N.T. Wright
books.rationale: Wright provides a great first commentary experience for high school students.  The familiar tone and approach is very accessible and helpful as a companion read to the Gospel of Luke.
books.sharedText:
New Testament: Grade 9
New Testament: Grades 10-11
New Testament: Grade 12

books.title: When the Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers
books.author: Marcellino D'Ambrosio
books.rationale: The first half of the book contains biographies from early church history covered in this rotation.

books.title: Turning Points in American Church History
books.author: Elesha J. Coffman
books.rationale: An insightful look at some key moments leading up to, during, and following our current history rotation to give more context and perspective on how these events have impacted the American church.
books.scopeText:
Term 1 (Partial Term)
Term 2 (Partial Term)
Term 3 (Partial Term)

books.title: Reading the Bible for a Change
books.author: Ray Lubeck
books.rationale: Ray Lubeck’s Reading the Bible for a Change teaches readers how to approach the bible and how to relate it to our lives. Through careful study, a student will learn how to approach the various genres of the Old and New Testaments, as well as how to respond and apply them to one’s life.

books.title: God of All Things
books.author: Andrew Wilson
books.rationale: In God of All Things, pastor and author Andrew Wilson invites you to rediscover God through ordinary, everyday things. He explores the idea of a material world and presents a variety of created marvels that reveal the gospel in everyday life and fuel worship and joy in God.

books.title: Atomic Habits
books.author: James Clear
books.rationale: James Clear, one of the world's leading experts on habit formation, reveals practical strategies that will teach you exactly how to form good habits, break bad ones, and master the tiny behaviors that lead to remarkable results. This book brings Mason's thoughts on habits into the 21st century applying current research in this field.
books.sharedText:
Econ/Civics: Grade 10 (U.S.)
Econ/Civics: Grade 10 (Canada)

books.title: Think: The Life of the Mind and the Love of God
books.author: John Piper
books.rationale: A focus on using both heart and mind to know and love God and care for others.
books.sharedText:
Econ/Civics: Grade 10 (U.S.)
Econ/Civics: Grade 10 (Canada)

books.title: Reading PDF
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: Students will learn about important historical documents developed between 1800-1900. Following the founding of the country, the framework of the nation begins to take shape. The Missouri Compromise sets the stage for the Civil War as new states join the nation; the federal government experiences internal conflict as the Executive Branch disregards the Judicial Branch over the sovereignty of tribal nations; and the Reconstruction Amendments are added to the Constitution.  A "Reading PDF" will be included in the lesson plans that provides accessible analysis integrated with relevant excerpts of Supreme Court decisions, legislative acts, and other texts as appropriate.
books.sharedText:
American Gov./ Economics: Grade 8
Plutarch/Government: Grade 9 (U.S.)
Econ/Civics: Grade 10 (U.S.)
Econ/Civics: Grade 11 (U.S.)

books.title: What Would Keynes Do?
books.author: Tejvan Pettinger
books.rationale: An easy-to-follow introduction to economics and how it applies to real-life decisions from a variety of economic theory approaches.
books.sharedText:
Econ/Civics: Grade 10 (U.S.)
Econ/Civics: Grade 10 (Canada)
Econ/Civics: Grade 11 (U.S.)
Econ/Civics: Grade 11 (Canada)

books.title: Ourselves Book 2: Self-Direction
books.author: Charlotte Mason
books.rationale: Mason emphasized that children are persons; this is the volume in which she defines that term. What characteristics do we, as Image-Bearers, have in common? What kinds of pitfalls should we anticipate? These are some of the profound questions Mason deals with in this, her only volume written directly to students.
books.sharedText:
Ethics: Part 4
Ethics: Part 5

This book brings Mason's thoughts on habits into the 21st century applying current research in this field.

books.title: The Prime Ministers: Canada's Leaders and the Nation They Shaped
books.author: J.D.M. Stewart
books.rationale: The Prime Ministers is a journey through Canada's political history through a chronological survey of its twenty-four Prime Ministers. Written specifically for high school students (and adults) to provide them with deeper knowledge of our nation's history, this book is less a collection of biographical sketches, and more a series of profiles focused on the defining events, policy decision, visions for the country, character strengths and weaknesses, missteps and triumphs, and leadership style of each Prime Minister.
books.sharedText:
Econ/Civics: Grade 10 (Canada)

books.title: Caesar's English II
books.author: Michael Clay Thompson
books.rationale: Studies in Latin stems, alongside typical classical and academic vocabulary words, will aid students in using, spelling and understanding these words in context.

books.title: Bryson's Dictionary of Troublesome Words: A Writer's Guide to Getting It Right
books.author: Bill Bryson
books.rationale: A helpful resource for avoiding error and increasing clarity in composition.

books.title: Style: The Basics of Clarity & Grace 5th ed.
books.author: Joseph Williams, Joseph Bizup
books.rationale: This classic work covers elemental principles of writing that will help students diagnose the strengths and weaknesses of their prose quickly and revise effectively. The text is written in a conversational style, offering reason-based principles for clear writing.

books.title: Our Work: Grades 9-12
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This is a compilation of the year's Recitation, Picture Study, Hymns, Spirituals, and Folk Songs. Line drawings of artifacts from the historical time period are also included to provide students with inspiration for their Book of Centuries. We recommend printing or purchasing a copy for each student.
books.sharedText:
Recitation: Grade 9
Recitation: Grade 10
Recitation: Grade 11
Recitation: Grade 12
Music: Level 5
Picture Study

books.title: Across This Land
books.author: John C Hudson
books.rationale: This book provides a narrative regional study of North America for our high school students.
books.sharedText:
Geography: Grade 10-11
Geography: Grade 12

books.title: Five Months on the Missouri River: Paddling a Dugout Canoe
books.author: Thomas Elpel
books.rationale: You are on the river with Thomas and crew as they explore the Missouri from source to St. Louis in the reverse footsteps of Lewis and Clark noting what has changed and what remains the same.
books.sharedText:
Geography: Grade 9
Geography: Grade 10-11
Geography: Grade 12

books.title: Sufferings in Africa: The Astonishing Account of a New England Sea Captain Enslaved by North African Arabs
books.author: Captain James Riley
books.rationale: A true tale of shipwreck that a young Abraham Lincoln read.
books.sharedText:
Geography: Grade 10-11
Geography: Grade 12

books.title: All Over the Map: A Cartographic Odyssey
books.author: Betsy Mason & Greg Miller
books.rationale: A collection of amazing maps covering a wide range of topics.  Students will be inspired to make maps of their own.

books.title: Reading American Horizons
books.author: Various Authors
books.rationale: From sources like Andrew Jackson’s “Veto of the Bank of the United States" to “the Declaration of Sentiments,” students will get their hands on written and visual documents that will help them see firsthand what people in the 1800s were encountering on a day-to-day basis. Students will develop their historical thinking skills by using these primary sources.

books.title: The Narrative of Lunsford Lane, Formerly of Raleigh, N. C.
books.author: Lunsford Lane
books.rationale: Originally published in 1842, this autobiography of Lunsford Lane details his life of slavery and his eventual escape using a unique method, purchasing his own freedom. To do this, he would need to have help from others around him. Lunsford Lane faced many hardships and took on entire judicial systems in his quest for his and his family’s freedom. Students, when reading The Narrative of Lunsford Lane, will encounter the real-life story of a brave man who lost his home but freed himself and his family.

books.title: American History, Volume 1: 1492-1877
books.author: Thomas Kidd
books.rationale: In American History: Volumes 1 and 2, Thomas Kidd tells the story of the United States from early American encounters to the modern-day American culture in an engaging and fresh way. Thomas Kidd uses primary sources and other scholarly resources to introduce the student to the people and places that have great historical significance—whether good or bad.

books.title: They Fought Like Demons: Women Soldiers in the Civil War
books.author: De Anne Blanton & Lauren M. Cook
books.rationale: Most people will never know the truth behind people like Albert Cashier, Frances Clayton, or the countless other aliases that women took on so that they could join the fight for causes that they believed in. The authors dive into military archives with the goal to uncover the many stories of women who stepped into the role of a male soldier in the 1800s. And with engaging chapters and well-rounded research, the student will be able to see the war through the eyes of women as they took to the battlefield alongside brothers, sons, fathers, and husbands. These women would come to be known later on—courtesy of documents from soldiers—as soldiers who fought like demons.

books.title: American History, Volume 2: 1877 - Present
books.author: Thomas Kidd
books.rationale: In American History: Volumes 1 and 2, Thomas Kidd tells the story of the United States from early American encounters to the modern-day American culture in an engaging and fresh way. Thomas Kidd uses primary sources and other scholarly resources to introduce the student to the people and places that have great historical significance—whether good or bad.

books.title: I Am a Man: Chief Standing Bear's Journey for Justice
books.author: Joe Starita
books.rationale: This book is about Chief Standing Bear’s fight for justice as he navigates the loss of his land—taken by manipulation and coercion. Joe Starita uses primary sources to help the audience see many of the hardships that Chief Standing Bear went through — like the loss of his daughter and brother —and how others came to fight for him and his people. It grounds the reader in the time and experiences of another people group who were struggling to find their identity in a new nation.

books.title: When America First Met China
books.author: Eric Jay Dolin
books.rationale: This is the story of trade between nations. One nation has just been born from a bloody battle with Britain, and one nation is steeped in a 2000-year tradition. The United States of America has to break away from using its mother nation for trading, and as it navigates trade with China, there will be collisions, barriers, and failings, but there will also be triumphs. China’s culture is also explored in great depth as Dolin uses stories of real people who broke the rules and recounted all the different happenings taking place in this nation.

books.title: Erebus: The Story of a Ship
books.author: Michael Palin
books.rationale: Erebus is a book about exploration and mystery. Palin takes the student into two of the most harrowing exploration trips ever recorded, the last trip being unsuccessful and leading to a mystery that still puzzles modern-day minds. By reading Erebus, students will get an account of what is known about the first ever explorations into Antarctica, the most isolated continent in the world.
books.sharedText:
World History: Grade 9
World History: Grades 10-12

books.title: Blood and Iron: The Rise and Fall of the German Empire
books.author: Katja Hoyer
books.rationale: Katja Hoyer is a German-British historian and expert in the field of Germany’s history. She brings that expertise to the pages of her book which engages with the people, policies, and places that helped build the German Empire and ultimately led to its downfall. This book offers the student the history of a nation whose national pride led to two World Wars.
books.sharedText:
World History: Grades 10-12
World History: Grade 9

books.title: Christians Reading Classics
books.author: Nadya Williams
books.rationale: Christianity began in a pagan land with its own myths and writings about gods, villains, and heroes. These stories would become ingrained in the culture. Stories from Homer and writings from Plato and Aristotle would impact the Greco-Roman world and still impact our world.  Williams explores the importance of understanding the content of these classics and the way they impacted the world, as Christians.

books.title: Ancient Empires: From Mesopotamia to the Rise of Islam
books.author: Eric H. Cline & Mark W. Graham
books.rationale: Ancient Empires is a relatively brief yet comprehensive and even-handed overview of the ancient Near East, the Mediterranean, and Europe, including the Greco-Roman world, Late Antiquity, and the early Muslim period.

books.title: Early Christian Writings: The Apostolic Fathers edited
books.author: Andrew Louth
books.rationale: With the birth of Christianity came a lot of writings from a lot of different authors. In this collection, authors like Clement, Ignatius, Barnabas, and Polycarp contribute to the growth of Christianity and write documents against heresies, trying to answer questions of philosophy and questions about their God.

books.title: Sophie's Rebellion
books.author: Beverley Boissery
books.rationale: Sophie's Rebellion is a fictional account of one American girl's experience of the 1837-1838 Rebellions, as she is held prisoner alongside her British travelling companions and befriends a young rebel named Luc. Through Sophie's eyes, the reader is able to see multiple perspectives of this conflict and is introduced to a number of notable historical figures. A trained historian, Beverley Boissery creates an authentically detailed account of Upper and Lower Canada in the mid-nineteenth century.
books.sharedText:
Canadian History: Grades 9-10
Canadian History: Grades 7-8

books.title: Early Voices
books.author: Mary Alice Downie
books.rationale: This collection of writings by 29 different women presents a kaleidoscope image of Canada's history. The wide range of individual voices, backgrounds, and histories provided by these primary documents complement the other historical non-fiction and fiction read during the year.
books.sharedText:
Canadian History: Grades 11-12
Canadian History: Grades 9-10

books.title: The Underground Railroad: Next Stop, Toronto!
books.author: Adrienne Shadd, Afua Cooper, & Karolyn Smardz Frost
books.rationale: The Underground Railroad: Next Stop, Toronto! is a detailed account of Black history in Canada focusing on the stories of those who made the dangerous journey north to freedom. Using a wide variety of primary sources, including archival documents, newspaper articles, letters, photographs, maps, archeological artifacts, and more, this book recounts what happened to those freedom-seekers once they arrived safely and settled. Through this lens, readers are also introduced to what life was like in the city of Toronto in the nineteenth century. The presence and contribution of Black people who made their homes in Toronto contributed significantly to the history and development of the city.

books.title: Red Wolf
books.author: Jennifer Dance
books.rationale: A harrowing and heartbreaking story of the impact of the Indian Act of 1876 on Indigenous families and communities through one child's experience of the Indian Residential School system. This novel is beautifully written and rich in historical detail, presenting an honest introduction to a dark chapter of Canada’s history.

books.title: Vanished Beyond the Map
books.author: Adam Shoalts
books.rationale: Vanished Beyond the Map unfolds like a mystery novel: after first stumbling upon the name Hubert Darrell in an old encyclopedia, Adam Shoalts sets off on a journey through the arctic and the archives to discover as much as he can about this nearly-forgotten explorer, mapmaker and guide who disappeared in 1910. This book is remarkable for showing readers what is involved in trying to solve a historical mystery: where do you find clues? what kinds of questions do you ask? how much can we really know about the past? But it is also a book which situates Darrell within the wider world of geographers and explorers in the late-nineteenth century, who were attempting to complete the map of the world by charting the unknown lands of the arctic and antarctic.
books.sharedText:
Geography: Grade 8 (Canada)
Canadian History: Grades 9-10

books.title: The Charlottetown Conference and the Birth of Confederation
books.author: Deirdre Kessler
books.rationale: The Charlottetown Conference and the Birth of Confederation is a detailed, engagingly-written account of the historical factors, issues, political players, and conferences which eventually led to Canada's Confederation in 1867. Attention is paid to the motives and pressures urging British North America towards unity, as well as the specific interests and context of each of the colonies before Confederation. The book brings this historical period to life with numerous photographs, paintings, archival documents, and other primary sources.
books.sharedText:
Canadian History: Grades 7-8
Canadian History: Grades 9-10

books.title: When America First Met China
books.author: Eric Jay Dolin
books.rationale: This is the story of trade between nations. One nation has just been born from a bloody battle with Britain, and one nation is steeped in a 2000-year tradition. The United States of America has to break away from using its mother nation for trading, and as it navigates trade with China, there will be collisions, barriers, and failings, but there will also be triumphs. China’s culture is also explored in great depth as Dolin uses stories of real people who broke the rules and recounted all the different happenings taking place in this nation.

books.title: Erebus: The Story of a Ship
books.author: Michael Palin
books.rationale: Erebus is a book about exploration and mystery. Palin takes the student into two of the most harrowing exploration trips ever recorded, the last trip being unsuccessful and leading to a mystery that still puzzles modern-day minds. By reading Erebus, students will get an account of what is known about the first ever explorations into Antarctica, the most isolated continent in the world.
books.sharedText:
World History: Grade 9
World History: Grades 10-12

books.author: Katja Hoyer

She brings that expertise to the pages of her book which engages with the people, policies, and places that helped build the German Empire and ultimately led to its downfall. This book offers the student the history of a nation whose national pride led to two World Wars.
books.sharedText:
World History: Grades 10-12
World History: Grade 9

books.author: Nadya Williams
books.rationale: Christianity began in a pagan land with its own myths and writings about gods, villains, and heroes. These stories would become ingrained in the culture. Stories from Homer and writings from Plato and Aristotle would impact the Greco-Roman world and still impact our world.  Williams explores the importance of understanding the content of these classics and the way they impacted the world, as Christians.

books.title: Ancient Empires: From Mesopotamia to the Rise of Islam
books.author: Eric H. Cline & Mark W. Graham
books.rationale: Ancient Empires is a relatively brief yet comprehensive and even-handed overview of the ancient Near East, the Mediterranean, and Europe, including the Greco-Roman world, Late Antiquity, and the early Muslim period.

books.title: Early Christian Writings: The Apostolic Fathers edited
books.author: Andrew Louth
books.rationale: With the birth of Christianity came a lot of writings from a lot of different authors. In this collection, authors like Clement, Ignatius, Barnabas, and Polycarp contribute to the growth of Christianity and write documents against heresies, trying to answer questions of philosophy and questions about their God.

books.title: Cambridge Latin Unit 4 5th Ed.
books.author: Cambridge School Classics Project
books.rationale: This is the student book. Includes a selection of original texts by Roman authors (adapted where necessary), including Catullus, Ovid, Pliny, Tacitus, and Virgil.

books.title: Cambridge Latin Unit 4 Online Resources
books.rationale: Site contains free cultural background information. Elevate/Cambridge Go is a subscription service for online games and practice.

books.title: Cambridge Latin Units 1-4 Teacher Manual (1 Year Digital Access)
books.author: Cambridge School Classics Project
books.rationale: Contains important teaching tips.
books.sharedText:
Latin 1 (Independent Study)
Latin 2 (Independent Study)
Latin 3
Latin 4

books.title: A Simply Healthy Life
books.author: Caroline Fausel
books.rationale: Health coach and wellness expert Caroline Fausel introduces her readers to simple strategies for living a healthy life. From fueling our bodies and integrating exercise to using spiritual practices like Sabbath rest and setting up a system for habits, Caroline lays out the steps needed to live a healthy, fulfilled life.

books.title: Body into Balance: An Herbal Guide to Holistic Self-Care
books.author: Maria Groves
books.rationale: Herbalist Maria Noel Groves opens the door to natural wellness by taking the reader on a journey through the major body systems - respiratory, digestive, immune, nervous, memory, circulatory and more - and showing readers how to support those body systems with natural remedies.

books.title: The Joy of Movement
books.author: Kelly McGonigal
books.rationale: Kelly McGonigal, PhD, research psychologist, lecturer at Stanford University and award winning science writer, blends her passion for science and her talent for storytelling and illustrates to readers how movement is integral to both our happiness and our humanity. Readers walk away with insight from neuroscience, psychology, anthropology and biology to see how movement is intertwined with some of the most basic human joys and they learn why it is a powerful antidote to the modern epidemics of depression, anxiety and loneliness.

books.title: Eat Well and Feel Great
books.author: Tina Lond-Caulk
books.rationale: Nutritionist Tina Lond-Caulk provides teens with an easy to use evidence based handbook filled with practical tips, recipes and illustrations that make the art of eating well an achievable goal.

books.title: The Ultimate Sashiko Sourcebook
books.author: Susan Briscoe
books.rationale: Students will use the illustrations and directions in the book to learn Sashiko techniques and designs.

books.title: Frankenstein
books.author: Mary Shelley
books.rationale: A famous gothic novel first published in 1818 and then revised in 1831, Frankenstein influenced the development of science fiction. This gripping story was composed as a result of a writing challenge when the author was only 19, and it revolves around personhood, conscience, and the ethics of scientific ambition, themes which are especially important for students to wrestle with in today's age of AI.

books.title: Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass
books.author: Frederick Douglass
books.rationale: Students read extended selections of this classic 19th century narrative which details the author's story from slavery to freedom. For US students, this text will enhance their reading of Lincoln & Douglass in US History.
books.sharedText:
General Lit: Grades 7-8
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: The Souls of Black Folk
books.author: W.E.B. Dubois
books.rationale: This landmark text of American literature and sociology is paired with Up from Slavery by Booker T. Washington in the lesson plans. This pairing immerses students in the dialogues around the turn of the century as they read about Washington's life and aspirations and then consider Dubois's response.
books.sharedText:
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: Up from Slavery
books.author: Booker T. Washington
books.rationale: This is a first-person account of what it was like to be born on a slave plantation, live through the Civil War and Reconstruction, and make a new life as a free person. Washington's accomplishments and sense of hope are inspiring, and his founding of the Tuskegee Institute which continues as a historically black university and National Historic Site today is a remarkable achievement. Lesson plans pair Dubois's response to Washington to allow students a richer experience of the discussions of the times.
books.sharedText:
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: Silas Marner
books.author: George Eliot (Mary Ann Evans)
books.rationale: Students spend two terms lingering with this nineteenth-century novel which depicts British village life while stretching the reader's moral imagination. Composition lessons build on these lessons by asking students to analyze characters, compare characters’ development during the two time periods of the novel, and use the events of the novel to discuss ethical questions.

books.title: Short stories and other writings
books.author: Various
books.rationale: Lesson plans curate a selection of writings from a variety of 19th century authors such as Pushkin, O.Henry, Tagore, and others to give students exposure to a broader range of genres, authors, and ideas.
books.sharedText:
Lit./Mythology: Grades 7-8
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: English Literature for Boys and Girls
books.author: H.E. Marshall
books.rationale: A delightful introduction to the writers of English literature whose works hold the greatest appeal for the youthful reader. The life and personality of each author is given in outline, with enough material quoted from key works to give an idea of the text.

books.title: History of Literature PDF
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: Curated selections of readings on literary developments, significant authors, and primary sources from the 19th century. Students use this PDF along with selected links and readings in Marshall's and Long's spines to deepen their relationship with 19th-century literature.

books.title: American Literature
books.author: William Long
books.rationale: A helpful chronological look at American Literature and authors.

books.title: The Tempest (Audio recording)
books.author: Arkangel
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8
Plays: Grades 9-12

books.title: The Tempest
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.rationale: This play is often called a Tragicomedy because it has both comedic and tragic elements. It is about an overthrown Duke and his daughter who are exiled on an island. The play addresses the themes of power, betrayal, magic, slavery, and the restoration of relationships.
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8
Plays: Grades 9-12

books.title: Henry IV Part 2 (Audio recording)
books.author: Arkangel
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8
Plays: Grades 9-12

books.title: Henry IV Part 2
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.rationale: This history play is a tale about Prince Hal falling into his father's footsteps as king. Hal's life will no longer be the same when he feels the weight of the crown.
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8
Plays: Grades 9-12

books.title: The Winter's Tale
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.rationale: The Winter's Tale is play divided into two halves—tragedy and redemption. It begins in the court of Leontes, King of Sicilia, whose sudden and irrational jealousy leads him to accuse his wife Hermione of betrayal. His actions cause devastating consequences, including the loss of family, friendship, and trust. This play emphasizes the power of jealousy to destroy, but the greater power of love to restore.
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8
Plays: Grades 9-12

books.title: The Winter's Tale (Audio recording)
books.author: Arkangel
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8
Plays: Grades 9-12

books.title: The Word Made Fresh
books.author: Abram Van Engen
books.rationale: Van Engen lays out a beautiful relational approach to poetry which resonates deeply with Mason. As he models a love for poetry and gives insights into how to enjoy, understand, and read poetry (or start on the journey!), students will continue growing their own delight. Lesson plans pair readings from the book with practical exercises.
books.sharedText:
Poetry Study: Grade 10
Poetry Study: Grade 11

books.title: Poetry Anthology
books.author: Various
books.rationale: Students use a poetry anthology of their choice (in print or online) for the exercises accompanying readings in The Word Made Fresh. Suggestions are listed in Extra Helpings for the course, but any anthology with a wide variety of poems and poets is sufficient.
books.sharedText:
Poetry Study: Grade 10
Poetry Study: Grade 11

books.title: Poetry
books.author: Various
books.rationale: Lesson plans draw in additional poetry from the 19th century to broaden the student's encounter with poetry from this time period.

books.title: The Lightning Dreamer: Cuba's Greatest Abolitionist
books.author: Margarita Engle
books.rationale: This book is a poetic fictionalized account of famous Cuban abolitionist and author Gertrudis Gómez Avellaneda (1814-1873). Since Avellaneda wrote in Spanish, this biography allows students to get a glimpse of a significant 19th century author outside the English canon. Engle's style also provides opportunities for students to consider the differences between poetry and prose and experiment with point of view in connected composition assignments.
books.sharedText:
Lit./Mythology: Grades 7-8
Poetry Reading: Grades 9-12

books.title: The Crescent Moon
books.author: Rabindranath Tagore
books.rationale: This delightful collection of children's poetry was translated from Bengali into English by Tagore himself. Lesson plans will draw from this collection and include links to additional poetry by this famous 19th century author.
books.sharedText:
Poetry: Grades 1-4
Poetry: Grades 5-6
Poetry Reading: Grade 7
Poetry Reading: Grade 8
Poetry Reading: Grades 9-12

books.title: The Aeneid
books.author: Virgil
books.rationale: The famous epic poem in which Virgil seeks to outdo Homer and build the glory of Rome. This text complements this year's studies in ancient history which cover the Roman period.

books.title: Jump Back, Paul: The Life and Poems of Paul Laurence Dunbar
books.author: Sally Derby
books.rationale: An engaging narrative telling about the life and times of Paul Laurence Dunbar, one of the most widely acclaimed American poets at the end of the 19th century. Dunbar's poetry is woven throughout the narrative so students can understand its context and significance.
books.sharedText:
Poetry: Grades 5-6
Poetry Reading: Grade 7
Poetry Reading: Grade 8
Poetry Reading: Grades 9-12

books.title: Algebra and Trigonometry: Functions and Applications
books.author: Paul Foerster
books.rationale: A master teacher guides you through the next level of algebra and trig.

books.title: Denison Algebra 2
books.author: Denison
books.rationale: Denison offers a complete high school math program with video lessons taught by a veteran teacher and homeschool dad for Pre-Algebra through Algebra 2. Pre-algebra only requires familiarity with the four basic functions (add, subtract, multiply, and divide). After Pre-Algebra, the course splits into two tracks for Algebra, Geometry, and Algebra 2: a track for students who don't have significant struggles but aren't enjoying math, and a Success track for students who struggle with math and need shorter lessons with more scaffolding.

books.title: Primary French Course Part I
books.author: Otto Siepmann
books.rationale: This book teaches vocabulary and grammar through reading, writing, and spoken exercises.

books.title: Mussorgsky's Pictures at an Exhibition
books.author: Anna Harwell Celenza
books.rationale: This book tells the sad but touching story behind Mussorgysky's famous composition to honor his friend's paintings.

books.title: Song in a Rainstorm: The Story of Musical Prodigy Thomas "Blind Tom" Wiggins
books.author: Glenda Armand
books.rationale: "Had he been born in our time," the author writes, "he might have been called an autistic musical savant." Students will enjoy this picture book biography of an incredible American performer and composer during the last half of the 19th century.

books.title: Our Hymns, Our Heritage
books.author: David & Barbara Leeman
books.rationale: This student hymnal contains multi-denominational hymns organized by the church year, with brief histories of the music and lyrics, bios of the writers, related Scripture, and other ideas to meditate upon.

books.title: Like a Bird: The Art of the American Slave Song
books.author: Cynthia Grady
books.rationale: This is a collection of African American spirituals, along with their stories and beautiful illustrations.

books.title: Fifty Steps in Sight-Singing
books.author: Arthur Somervell
books.rationale: Classic text to teach sight-singing.
books.sharedText:
Sol-fa: Level 3
Sol-fa: Level 4
Sol-fa: Level 5

books.title: The Ultimate Homeschool Physical Education Game Book
books.author: Guy Bailey
books.rationale: This book contains time-honored and newer playground games and skill-building activities for organized sports.
books.sharedText:
Playground Games
Sports

books.title: The Ultimate Homeschool Physical Education Game Book
books.author: Guy Bailey
books.rationale: This book contains time-honored and newer playground games and skill-building activities for organized sports.
books.sharedText:
Playground Games
Sports

books.title: 303 Tween-Approved Exercises and Active Games
books.author: Kimberly Wechsler
books.rationale: This book provides a great list of exercises that includes strength training, flexibility exercises, and cardiovascular activities. This can be used in a group or with one student.

books.title: Michael Faraday's The Chemical History of a Candle
books.author: Bill Hammack & Don DeCoste
books.rationale: This book introduces modern readers to Michael Faraday’s great nineteenth-century lectures on "The Chemical History of a Candle." Students will be watching the lectures on video, so this text is for teachers or for students who would like to follow along using the text.

books.title: Introductory Chemistry: A Foundation
books.author: Steven S. Zumdahl & Donald J. DeCoste
books.rationale: Masters at inspiring and engaging students, the authors of this best-selling text combine problem-solving strategies, early coverage of chemical reactions, accessible explanations and visualizations, and an emphasis on everyday applications to facilitate understanding.

books.title: The Chemical Elements Coloring and Activity Book
books.author: Ellen Johnston McHenry
books.rationale: All 118 elements are included in coloring pages, with information about its discovery, examples of molecules it will form, and pictures of how the element might be used in industry, medicine, the military, research, and fine arts.  Also included are a personalizable periodic table and a variety of games.

books.title: Alveary Chemistry Lab Book
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This document contains all of the instructions, procedures, and guidance needed to complete the laboratory component of High School Chemistry.

books.title: The Biology Coloring Book
books.author: Robert D. Griffin
books.rationale: In The Biology Coloring Book, students learn about the basic processes of life and the interactions of living things within their environment.

books.title: General Biology, 2nd Edition
books.author: Heather Ayala & Katie Rogstad
books.rationale: In a conversational tone and with reverence, the authors of this general biology text clearly guide students from the molecules of biology to the complexities of the human body and evolution.

books.title: The Fool and the Heretic
books.author: Todd Wood & Darrel Falk
books.rationale: Two scientists with differing perspectives dialogue respectfully about creation and evolution.  This is an excellent choice for students in contexts that perceive faith and science to be antagonistic, as well as those interested in learning to have healthy discussion with those of differing viewpoints.

books.title: The Spirit of the Rainforest
books.author: Dr. Rosa Vasquez Espinoza
books.rationale: A leading scientist explores the synergy between indigenous knowledge and classical science in this courageous and deeply spiritual adventure. This is an excellent choice for students who are secure in the harmony between faith and science and would benefit from a living example in their modern world, as well as those interested in indigenous voices.

books.title: Alveary Biology Lab Book
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This document contains all of the instructions, procedures, and guidance needed to complete the laboratory component of High School Biology.

books.title: Salt, Fat, Acid, Heat: Mastering the Elements of Good Cooking
books.author: Samin Nosrat
books.rationale: Whether for afternoon occupations or High School Food Science, young cooks will be inspired by this well-written text with its engaging narrative and beautiful illustrations.  The author does a good job of introducing the basic ingredients of cooking without requiring a full chemistry course; students who have already taken chemistry will find additional connections to enhance their understanding even more.
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)
Food Science Lessons

books.title: The Elements of Baking
books.author: Katarina Cermelj
books.rationale: This title is ideal for learning the science of baking, in which students will learn about and observe the properties of their ingredients, experiment with substitutions, and work toward their desired product.

books.title: Gullah Geechee Home Cooking: Recipes from the Matriarch of Edisto Island
books.author: Emily Meggett

books.title: Culinary Reactions: The Everyday Chemistry of Cooking
books.author: Simon Quellan Field
books.rationale: The author explains the chemistry behind a variety of culinary methods.

books.title: The Boy Who Harnessed the Wind, Young Reader's Edition
books.author: William Kamkwamba & Bryan Mealer
books.rationale: This wonderful story demonstrates the beauty, humanity, and accessibility of the engineering mindset.

books.title: The Things We Make
books.author: Bill Hammack, Ph.D
books.rationale: From medieval cathedrals to modern soda cans, this book will help learners grasp 'the engineering method' and notice it working all around them.

books.title: The Boy Who Harnessed the Wind
books.author: William Kamkwamba & Bryan Mealer
books.rationale: This wonderful story demonstrates the beauty, humanity, and accessibility of the engineering mindset.  This original version contains some sensitive content, which enriches the reader's understanding of the author's culture and challenges, but may not be appropriate for all learners.

books.title: The Way Things Work: Newly Revised Edition: The Ultimate Guide to How Things Work
books.author: David Macaulay
books.rationale: This well-written and humorously illustrated classic guides readers through the fundamental principles of machines and shows how the developments of the past are building blocks of the world of tomorrow.

books.title: By Design: Ethics, Theology, and the Practice of Engineering
books.author: Brad J. Kallenberg
books.rationale: An often overlooked topic, this text challenges students to consider the social context and formative nature of design.

books.title: Botany in a Day
books.author: Thomas Elpel
books.rationale: A patterned approach to learning the plant families.

books.title: What a Plant Knows
books.author: Daniel Chamovitz
books.rationale: A plant knows more than you think! This piece of botany literature provides a fascinating look at plants.

books.title: The Milkweed Lands: An Epic Story of One Plant: Its Nature and Ecology
books.author: Eric Lee-Mader
books.rationale: Delve into the fascinating role of this often-overlooked plant in a wide variety of ecosystems.  A collaboration between a noted ecologist and an award-winning botanical illustrator, this text will inspire readers in their own special studies.

books.title: Shanleya's Quest Card Game
books.author: Thomas Elpel
books.rationale: Botany in a Day author, Thomas Elpel, produced this deck of cards to help students begin to notice the patterns that are used to characterize common plant families.
books.sharedText:
Natural History: Grade 5
Botany Lessons

books.title: Growing a Revolution: Bringing Our Soil Back to Life
books.author: David Montgomery
books.rationale: Discussion of modern agriculture, the nature of soil, and how to sustainably steward the earth.
books.sharedText:
Botany Lessons
Microbiology Lessons

books.title: Elementary Studies in Plant Life
books.author: F.E. Fritsch & E. J. Salisbury
books.rationale: This is a hands-on look at many aspects of how plants work in a classic, digital text.  The text provides many lab activities for the course.

books.title: Tree Finder - Eastern US
books.author: May Watts

books.title: The Hidden Life of Trees: The Illustrated Edition
books.author: Peter Wohlleben
books.rationale: This title provides students with current scientific information and human connections to support their knowledge about trees both as individuals and within their context.  The beautiful photography in this piece of botany literature is inspiring and supportive.

books.title: The Sibley Guide to Trees
books.author: David Allen Sibley
books.rationale: Botany students use this guide to study tree families from across North America and to consider various aspects of tree anatomy.

books.title: Oaxaca Journal
books.author: Oliver Sacks
books.rationale: A master writer takes you on a trip to discover ferns in southern Mexico in this work of botany literature.

books.title: Master of Field Arts
books.author: Roseann Hanson
books.rationale: Welcome to the next level for nature journalers and field sketchers—a deep dive into becoming a dedicated master naturalist and field artist.

books.title: High School Handbook
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This manual covers everything from transcripts to testing and from college to trade careers (plus a whole lot more). Like an Alveary lesson plan, this book is simply a tool that is only effective when backed by Charlotte Mason’s philosophy of education.

books.title: The LEGO® Architect
books.author: Tom Alphin
books.rationale: Travel through the history of architecture in _The LEGO Architect_. Learn about styles like Art Deco, Modernism, and High-Tech, and find inspiration in galleries of LEGO models. Then take a turn building 12 models in a variety of styles.

books.title: Mastering Hand Building
books.author: Sunshine Cobb
books.rationale: From pinch pots to coiled boxes to soft slab tableware, mastering hand building is a lifelong pursuit. This book covers all the foundational skills, with lessons for constructing both simple and complex forms from clay.

books.title: GeoPuzzles
books.rationale: Kids will learn geography in a fun and engaging way, while discussing the different countries and how they are related.
